items.title: Object lifetime and ownership: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? Keeping a pointer variable alive keeps its pointed-to object alive as well.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Track object lifetime independently from pointer lifetime; a surviving pointer can be dangling after scope exit or free.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Track object lifetime independently from pointer lifetime; a surviving pointer can be dangling after scope exit or free.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Track object lifetime independently from pointer lifetime; a surviving pointer can be dangling after scope exit or free.
Keeping a pointer variable alive keeps its pointed-to object alive as well.

answer.explanation: Track object lifetime independently from pointer lifetime; a surviving pointer can be dangling after scope exit or free.

items.title: Object lifetime and ownership: executable application
items.prompt: Implement a function that stores each supplied integer in a function-local static object and returns its valid address, then print values from two sequential calls.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: static object lifetime without returning an automatic address

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises static object lifetime without returning an automatic address under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
